center.name: 让·路易·卡巴尼斯
center.summary: 让·路易·卡巴尼斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括Lesser jacana、Cinnamon-rumped trogon、Von der Decken's hornbill等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ash-breasted antbird (Myrmoborus lugubris) is an insectivorous bird in subfamily Thamnophilinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical antbirds". It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black bishop (Euplectes gierowii) is a species of passerine bird in the family Ploceidae native to Africa south of the Sahara. Three subspecies are recognised

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black manakin (Xenopipo atronitens) is a species of bird in the family Pipridae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, French Guiana, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blackish antbird (Cercomacroides nigrescens) is a species of passerine bird in subfamily Thamnophilinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical antbirds". It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, French Guiana, Guyana, Peru, and Suriname.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blue seedeater (Amaurospiza concolor) or Cabanis's seedeater is a species of bird in the cardinal family that is found in southern Mexico and Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The buff-throated tody-tyrant (Hemitriccus rufigularis) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The cinnamon-rumped foliage-gleaner (Philydor pyrrhodes) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in every mainland South American country except Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The cinnamon-rumped trogon (Harpactes orrhophaeus) is a species of bird in the family Trogonidae or Trogons, some of the most colourful birds on Earth, particularly the quetzals found in the Neotropics

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The creamy-bellied thrush (Turdus amaurochalinus) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, Peru, Uruguay, and as a vagrant to Chile and the Falkland Islands.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The golden-bellied flycatcher (Myiodynastes hemichrysus) is a passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Panama, Peru, and Venezuela.R

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The lesser jacana (Microparra capensis) is a wader in the family Jacanidae and can be found in Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Photographed in Ecuador The ocellated tapaculo (Acropternis orthonyx) is a large bird found in the northern Andes in South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rough-legged tyrannulet (Acrochordopus burmeisteri) is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The scalloped woodcreeper (Lepidocolaptes falcinellus) is a species of bird in the subfamily Dendrocolaptinae of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The slaty elaenia (Elaenia strepera) is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Colombia, Peru, and Venezuela, and as a vagrant in Ecuador and on Trinidad.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The spectacled bristle tyrant (Pogonotriccus orbitalis) is a species of passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The tit-like dacnis (Xenodacnis parina) is a small neotropical passerine bird found in southern Ecuador and Peru

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Von der Decken's hornbill (Tockus deckeni) is a hornbill found in East Africa, especially to the east of the East African Rift, from Ethiopia south to Tanzania

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The wren-like rushbird (Phleocryptes melanops) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Paraguay, Peru, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Andean slaty thrush (Turdus nigriceps) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Ecuador and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|250px|Rhodospiza obsoleta The desert finch (Rhodospiza obsoleta), sometimes called Lichtenstein's desert finch, is a large brown true finch found in southern Eurasia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pale-eyed pygmy tyrant (Atalotriccus pilaris) is a species of bird in the tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Guyana, Panama, and Venezuela.Clements, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=2016 |title=Fire-eyed Diucon Xolmis pyrope |volume=2016 |article-number=e.T22700007A93758538 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2016-3.RLTS.T22700007A93758538.en |access-date=11 May 2025}} | genus = Pyrope | parent_authority = Cabanis & Heine, 1860 | species = pyrope | authority = (Kittlitz, 1830) | synonyms = See text | range_map = Xolmis pyrope map.svg | range_map_caption = }} The fire-eyed diucon (Pyrope pyrope) is a passerine bird of South America belonging to the tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Argentina and Chile and as a vagrant to the Falkland Islands.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Taita fiscal or Teita fiscal (Lanius dorsalis) is a member of the shrike family found in east Africa from southeastern South Sudan, southern Ethiopia, and western Somalia to northeastern Tanzania.BirdLife International and Natureserve (2011) "Bird species distribution maps of the world." 2009

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Estrilda rufibarba |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22719566A263830041 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22719566A263830041.en |access-date=19 June 2025}} | taxon = Estrilda rufibarba | authority = (Cabanis, 1851) }} The Arabian waxbill (Estrilda rufibarba) is a highly sociable species of estrildid finch native to Yemen and south-western Saudi Arabia
